stories.headline: Zelensky fires army chief after protests push for front-line leadership change

sources.body:
After nationwide protests Oleksandr Syrskyi is to be replaced in reshuffle and popular defence minister returns to government

Volodymyr Zelenskyy has fired Ukraine’s commander-in-chief Oleksandr Syrskyi and offered a “prominent position” to the defence minister he sacked last week in a move that sparked nationwide protests .

In a late-night TV address, Zelenskyy said he was appointing Mykhailo Drapatyi, a popular and innovative general, as the new head of Ukraine’s armed forces. Drapatyi would oversee a reorganisation of the army’s command structure, Ukraine’s president said.

Zelenskyy said he was bringing Mykhailo Fedorov back into government after his removal as defence minister prompted thousands of people to demonstrate in Kyiv and other Ukrainian cities, in one of the biggest political crises since Russia’s 2022 invasion.

Ukraine’s president paid tribute to Syrskyi and credited him with leading the defence of Kyiv and launching a counter-invasion of Russia’s Kursk province in summer 2024, in which foreign tanks rolled on to Russian territory for the first time since the second world war.

“We have come a long way. The defence of Ukraine continues. And every soldier deserves to be treated with respect,” Zelenskyy said, adding that he was grateful to Syrskyi and to “every one of our soldiers” fighting on the frontline.

Zelenskyy will be hoping that his radical reshuffle of the army’s leadership will assuage protesters who have taken to the streets demanding Syrskyi’s removal. They had given a deadline of Friday for Fedorov to be reinstated and promised “indefinite protests” if their request was ignored.

Fedorov welcomed Drapatyi’s appointment. “This brings a breath of fresh air and new hope to the struggle of free people for freedom and justice. It is a voice for change that could not be ignored,” he said.

He added: “The high expectations of the Ukrainian people must be met with a clear vision for ending the war for independence, a strong team and decisive actions aimed at saving the lives of our soldiers, automating the frontline, delivering asymmetric strikes and crippling the Russian economy.”

Drapatyi, the new commander-in-chief, is well-regarded within Ukraine’s army and is liked by the public. He is viewed as an ally of Fedorov, whose attempts to reform the military and force it to adopt modern technology and data led to a bitter clash with the old-school Syrskyi.

A major general, Drapatyi is commander of Ukraine’s joint forces group. He has been fighting Russia since its covert attempt in 2014 to take over the eastern Donbas region. He became famous nationally when he drove an infantry fighting vehicle over a barricade set up by pro-Russian militants in the city of Mariupol.

The Ukrainian media outlet Babel described Drapatyi as one of Ukraine’s most capable battlefield commanders. It said his new job would be fundamentally about reforming a large institution rather than commanding troops in combat.

Zelenskyy said he and Drapatyi agreed that Ukraine’s long-range drone attacks on targets in Russia such as oil refineries would continue. “Our programme of precision strikes will be implemented with absolute precision,” he said, promising a smooth and coordinated handover following Syrskyi’s departure.
sources.headline: Zelenskyy fires Ukraine’s army chief and offers Fedorov ‘prominent position’

stories.headline: Iran and U.S. trade fresh strikes as Houthis threaten Saudi shipping lanes

sources.body: Item 4 of 4 A billboard depicting U.S. President Donald Trump and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on a building in Tehran, Iran, July 21, 2026.Majid Asgaripour/WANA (West Asia News Agency) via REUTERS [4/4] A billboard depicting U.S. President Donald Trump and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on a building in Tehran, Iran, July 21, 2026.Majid Asgaripour/WANA (West Asia News Agency) via REUTERS Purchase Licensing Rights , opens new tab Summary US and Iranian forces exchange strikes for 10th night in a row Iran-allied Houthis threaten naval blockade against Saudi Arabia Signs of diplomatic efforts to de-escalate conflict Iranian forces target US sites in Gulf and Jordan Iran's Revolutionary Guards say two tankers hit in Hormuz DUBAI, July 21 (Reuters) - U.S. forces bombed targets in southern Iran overnight after President Donald Trump said Tehran would pay ​dearly for the deaths of American soldiers, while Iran targeted U.S. sites in Bahrain, Kuwait and Jordan and a tanker was hit in the Strait of Hormuz. The exchanges ‌came a day after Yemen's Iran-aligned Houthis said they would impose a naval blockade on Saudi Arabia, opening a potential new front in the war and raising the threat to global energy supplies and trade beyond the Gulf. The Reuters Iran Briefing newsletter keeps you informed with the latest developments and analysis of the Iran war. Sign up here. Despite the escalating cycle of attacks that has all but wrecked an interim peace agreement signed last month, signs that Tehran and Washington want to resume diplomacy kept oil prices , opens new tab in check. U.S. Central Command said it had completed its latest round of strikes on ​Iran, saying it hit Iranian military command centers, maritime capabilities, missile and drone launch sites, and air defence systems. Explosions were heard around the city of Shiraz in southern Iran, while ​Konarak and Chabahar on the southern coast also came under attack, according to state media. Iran responded with renewed attacks across the Gulf, where recent strikes ⁠on desalination facilities have raised concerns about water shortages and underscored the growing threat to civilian infrastructure. Iran's Revolutionary Guards said they targeted infrastructure in Bahrain belonging to U.S. tech company Amazon in retaliation for ​what they described as a U.S. strike on the construction site of Iran's planned Darkhovin nuclear power plant. Iranian state media reported that Iranian forces also targeted accommodation facilities used by U.S. military personnel at ​Bahrain's Sheikh Isa Air Base. There was no immediate comment from the U.S. military, Bahrain or Amazon on the reported incidents, which Reuters was unable to verify. The Iranian army said it launched drone attacks against three U.S. military bases in Kuwait on Tuesday. The Revolutionary Guards said they attacked a U.S. military site in Jordan, and Jordan said its forces intercepted and destroyed five Iranian drones. TANKERS STRUCK The United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations agency said on Tuesday a ​tanker in the strait reported being struck by an unknown projectile, forcing its crew to abandon the vessel and board a lifeboat. Also on Tuesday, Iran's Revolutionary Guards said two oil tankers had caught fire ​after explosions while attempting to transit the southern route of the Strait of Hormuz. It was not immediately clear when those incidents took place. Iran had been pressing the Houthis to close the Bab el-Mandeb gateway to the ‌Red Sea ⁠if the U.S. continued to attack Iranian power infrastructure — putting two of the world's most vital energy arteries at risk. A full closure of Bab el-Mandeb would reduce global oil supply by 7% as it would leave most Saudi oil exports unable to leave the region . The disruption would add to the huge cut to oil flows from the war in the Gulf, which has already reduced shipments by 10% of global supply. After the Houthis announced their blockade, the Saudi-led coalition in Yemen said it began implementing protection measures for its ships moving through the Bab el-Mandeb Strait. Oil markets weighed the threat to shipping against reports ​of diplomatic efforts to contain the conflict, with ​benchmark Brent crude prices easing slightly in ⁠early trading. DIPLOMATIC PUSH TO RESTORE CEASEFIRE A senior Iranian official told Reuters on Monday that Tehran had received a proposal from mediators for a 10-day ceasefire in efforts to salvage the interim deal, intended to pave the way to a lasting agreement to end the war that began on February 28 with ​U.S.-Israeli attacks on Iran. Iranian Interior Minister Eskandar Momeni had asked Pakistan to resume its mediation role in the conflict. He later arrived in Islamabad ​for further talks. The latest phase ⁠of fighting has proved to be among the deadliest of the conflict for U.S. forces. U.S. troop deaths rose to 17 over the weekend. Trump, under mounting pressure at home over rising gasoline prices since the war began, said the latest U.S. strikes on Iran were honoring the deaths of American service members. "Every time Iran kills an American Soldier they will pay for that killing many times over! This directive has been passed on to Secretary ⁠of War, Pete ​Hegseth, Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, Daniel Caine, and every Leader in the Military," Trump wrote on Truth ​Social on Monday. The war has killed thousands of people, mostly in Iran and Lebanon. Parts of Lebanon remain under Israeli occupation following attacks on Israel by Hezbollah fighters, who said they were acting in solidarity with Tehran. Lebanese President Joseph Aoun is due ​to meet Trump at the White House on Tuesday to present a plan on how to disarm Iran-aligned Hezbollah and secure an Israeli withdrawal. Reporting by Reuters bureaux; Writing by Ros Russell; Editing by Philippa Fletcher Our Standards: The Thomson Reuters Trust Principles. , opens new tab Suggested Topics: Middle East X Facebook Linkedin Email Link Purchase Licensing Rights
sources.headline: Iranian forces target Bahrain and Kuwait after US hits Iran's south

sources.body:
To recap, the Houthis , who control northern and western Yemen including the coast at the mouth of the Red Sea, announced a naval blockade on Saudi Arabia on ⁠Monday.

The announcement already appears to be having an impact. Two tankers which had just loaded Saudi crude bound for China and India at Saudi Arabia’s Red Sea port of Yanbu made U-turns on Tuesday, heading towards the Suez Canal rather ‌than out through the Bab el-Mandeb into the Indian Ocean.

Assessing the situation, British maritime risk management group Vanguard said:

double quotation mark The developments represent the first confirmed changes to commercial tanker routing following the (Houthi) embargo and are likely to increase disruption to Saudi crude exports and regional shipping patterns.”

With the war shutting the strait of Hormuz, the Red Sea has served as the main alternate route out for millions of barrels of Saudi oil per day, diverted by pipeline through Yanbu.
sources.headline: Hegseth estimates rising cost of Iran war now at $37.5bn – as it happened

sources.body: The US military launched an 11th straight night of strikes on Iran early Wednesday, with President Donald Trump saying the US was "not finished" attacking the Middle Eastern nation. The strikes followed Tehran's attacks on Gulf states, and its Houthi allies threatening to blockade Saudi ports. Follow our liveblog for the latest developments.
sources.headline: Middle East live: US launches 11th night of Iran strikes as diplomacy falters

stories.headline: France becomes first EU country to bar under-15s from social media

sources.body:
Parliament votes to stop children using apps such as TikTok in policy championed by Emmanuel Macron

France’s parliament has approved a bill banning social media access for children under 15, making it the first EU country to bar children from apps such as TikTok.

The president, Emmanuel Macron, has championed the ban as a key reform of his final term in office and pledged to enforce it by September.

“France is leading the way in Europe when it comes to protecting our children and teenagers,” Macron said in a video posted on social media, hailing “a major step forward”.

He thanked members of parliament for backing the legislation on Tuesday.

“The Constitutional Council must now rule on it, and then it will be time to take action to make this measure a reality and protect our children online,” he added on X.

After approval by the Senate earlier on Tuesday, members of the National Assembly passed the bill by 279 votes to 81.

A growing number of countries are taking steps to restrict social media access amid multiplying warnings over its harmful effects on children.

The ban was to be introduced in two stages, with under-15s blocked from creating new accounts from 1 September. The ban would apply to existing accounts from January 2027, according to the legislation.

The digital minister, Anne Le Hénanff, said before the vote that the timeline was realistic, “because age-verification tools already exist” and others are still in the works, and the onus was on the platforms to impose the rule.

“For four months, all of us in France will have to prove our age,” she told journalists. “If someone is under 15, the account will be closed.”

The minister also gave assurances that users’ personal data would be protected.

Enforcing the measure by September would ensure that one provision of the bill – a ban on mobile phones in high schools – takes effect at the beginning of the French school year.

France’s public health watchdog last year said platforms such as TikTok, Snapchat and Instagram were harmful to adolescents, particularly girls, though this was not the sole reason for their declining mental health.

Lawmakers agreed on the need for regulation but the two chambers had disagreed on the approach.

French senators had opted for a two-tier system distinguishing between blacklisted platforms flagged as harmful to a child’s development, and those that could still be accessed with parental consent.

But the lower house’s blanket ban prevailed, despite criticism from some on the left over the age verification process, the speed of its implementation and risks of circumvention as well as privacy concerns.

Exceptions to the ban are provided for sites such as online encyclopedias and educational platforms, and the text does not provide for any penalties for children or parents.

“The major platforms are, in my view, largely ready,” assembly member Laure Miller told AFP.

The blacklisting system would have taken more time, including fresh consultations with the European Commission on the criteria, and carried a “small risk” of non-compliance with European law, said centrist senator Catherine Morin-Desailly.
sources.headline: France becomes first EU country to ban social media access for under-15s

sources.body: FRANCE 24's Clovis Casali reports from France's National Assembly, where lawmakers have approved a ban on social media for children and teenagers under-15, the first EU country to do so. FRANCE 24's Mark Owen speaks with Philippe Coen, lawyer and founder of the NGO Respect Zone.
sources.headline: French parliament backs social media ban for under-15s in EU first

stories.headline: Japan logs first official “cruelly hot” day as heatwave tops 40°C

sources.body:
In April Japan coined new term kokushobi for days above 40C as country advises workers to wear shorts to the office to cope with stifling heat

Japan logged its first official kokushobi or “cruelly hot day” on Tuesday since the new term for when temperatures top 40 degrees Celsius was introduced in April. The Japan Meteorological Agency coined the new designation to warn the public of the heat dangers of days that were once freak weather events but have now become more common.

The central Japan cities of Gujo and Tajimi in Gifu prefecture, along with Toyota in Aichi prefecture, all hit 40C. Gujo’s 40C and Toyota’s 40.1C were all-time highs recorded at their respective weather stations.

Across 914 weather stations nationwide, 278 registered temperatures exceeding 35C or moshobi – “extremely hot days,” the highest level of warning until the recent introduction of the new terminology. Days over 25C are called natsubi or “summer days”, while those exceeding 30C are known as manatsubi – “true summer days.”

On Monday, a man in his 40s was found dead on a street in Sanjo, Niigata prefecture, while another in his 70s died in Kanagawa, southwest of Tokyo. Both are believed to have succumbed to the heat.

Heatstroke deaths topped 2,000 for the first time in 2024, while more than 100,000 people were taken to hospital last year between the beginning of May and late September, according to the fire and disaster management agency.

The rising heat is affecting many aspects of Japanese life – including yields and the quality of rice, a staple. In response, farmers are planting more heat-tolerant varieties, while some are switching to crops such as avocado that have not traditionally been grown at scale in Japan.

Impacts are also being seen in sport, with the most high-profile being the restructuring of the hugely popular koshien national high school baseball tournament. Played in the sweltering heat of August, the games have been split into morning and evening sessions since 2024 to reduce the chances of heatstroke for both players and spectators.

Meanwhile, office workers are being encouraged to wear shorts on hot summer days, the latest relaxing of Japan’s buttoned-down corporate culture as part of “Cool Biz” measures to reduce electricity consumption through lower use of airconditioning. Previous initiatives have encouraged “salarymen” to shed jackets and ties, and wear short-sleeved shirts but this year’s expected scorching summer has prompted calls for another level of casualness.

Tokyo governor Yuriko Koike announced the new clothing recommendations for office wear which also include polo shirts, T-shirts and trainers where appropriate, against the backdrop of expected high temperatures and a “challenging outlook for electricity supply”.

A study by the National Institute for Environmental Studies (NIES) and Tokyo’s Waseda University in 2025 found that without drastic cuts to greenhouse gas emissions, school sports will become unplayable outdoors by the 2060s.

Average summer temperatures in Japan have risen about 1.3C over the past 100 years, according to the Japan Meteorological Agency (JMA). Both 2023 and 2024 were the hottest years since records began in 1898, but last year saw those levels surpassed again. The summer of 2025 was 2.4C above the average of the previous three decades, with 132 weather stations logging their highest ever average heat and a new all-time Japanese high of 41.8C at Isesaki, Gunma, north of Tokyo, on 5 August.
sources.headline: Japan records its first ‘cruelly hot day’ as cities swelter in 40-degree heat

stories.headline: Trump-aligned candidates sweep key Arizona GOP primaries

sources.body: To make it out of a Republican primary in Arizona, there’s one key litmus test: believing the 2020 election was stolen from Donald Trump. Election deniers who played prominent roles in efforts to overturn the 2020 results triumphed in statewide Republican primaries Tuesday, from Rep. Andy Biggs for governor to state Rep. Alexander Kolodin for secretary of state. State Senate President Warren Petersen was leading his primary for attorney general, though his race was not yet called as of early Wednesday morning. Biggs, a staunch Trump ally who helped try and overturn the election on Jan. 6, 2021, was later subpoenaed in an Arizona probe . Kolodin represented a slate of fake electors, leading to discipline from the Arizona state bar, and filed lawsuits to try to overturn both the 2020 and 2022 elections . Petersen launched a taxpayer-funded audit into Maricopa County’s election system that eventually led to a subpoena. Their victories show GOP primary voters continue to prefer candidates who have pushed false narratives about elections or worked to overturn them. But that can prove toxic in November — and Democrats plan to make their rivals pay for those positions in the fall. "It's one of those things that just won't go away,” said former Rep. Matt Salmon (R-Ariz.). “In the Republican Party in Arizona, in the primary, carrying the Trump line on virtually everything — and that includes election denialism — is still something that benefits you.” But in the general election, “it’s a stone around your neck,” Salmon said. “Among independents and moderate Republicans who vote in the general election, election denialism is not an asset.” Each of the triumphant election deniers Tuesday centered election integrity in their 2026 campaigns. Biggs has been a prominent supporter of the SAVE America Act, the stalled election bill that Trump has pushed repeatedly. Kolodin has advocated for ending Arizona’s permanent early voting list and for stringent ID requirements. And Petersen has pledged to expand the attorney general office’s election integrity unit in order to investigate election results. Arizona was one of the most competitive — and most contested — states in the 2020 election. Fox News’ decision to declare then-candidate Joe Biden’s victory in the state on election night sent shockwaves through Trump’s orbit. It took then-Republican Gov. Doug Ducey several weeks to acknowledge Biden won . The Grand Canyon State became ground zero for Trump’s effort to overturn the election. Trump and his allies pressured Arizona leaders, including then-House Speaker Rusty Bowers, to call the legislature back into session and recall the state’s slate of electors . A group of Republicans falsely posing as alternate electors were later charged with forgery, fraud and conspiracy, a case that is ongoing . Even still, running as an election denier hasn’t been a successful strategy for the Arizona GOP once November rolls around. In 2022, Arizona Republicans nominated hardline election deniers up and down the ballot — Kari Lake for governor, Blake Masters for U.S. Senate, and Mark Finchem for secretary of state — and saw losses in each race, even as Republicans nationally enjoyed an otherwise strong year. And in 2024, Lake again lost, this time for U.S. Senate, even as Trump won the state by more than five percentage points. “Biggs and Kolodin will be saddled with election denialism for the next three months,” said longtime Arizona GOP strategist Barrett Marson. “While Democrats would be wise to focus on affordability, certainly some of their talking points will focus on how these two are dangerous for democracy." Arizona Gov. Katie Hobbs, who ran unopposed in the Democratic primary Tuesday and will face Biggs in November, served as secretary of state during the 2020 election. She has portrayed this year’s gubernatorial election as a contrast between confidence and conspiracy in the state’s election system. “Arizona is one of seven swing states that will decide the presidential election,” Hobbs said during an MS NOW appearance Tuesday evening. “If Andy Biggs is governor, Arizona is the first swing state with an election denier in charge, and the time to stop that is not 2028. It is right now.” It’s almost impossible to be a Republican officeholder without agreeing with the president on the 2020 election and the need to dramatically change election law. “As long as Donald Trump, who has the bully pulpit and is the party leader … is talking about election integrity, it will be a top issue in GOP primaries,” said one Arizona Republican strategist, granted anonymity to discuss the issue candidly. “Election integrity measures are broadly popular, but the issue being front and center highlights divisions about Jan. 6th and wasn’t a winning general election issue in 2022,” the strategist continued. “Not sure that general election voters will care much about that in 2026 with such a focus on affordability issues. We are about to find out!” Trump, for his part, has proven unwilling to move past the 2020 election even after winning a return to the White House in 2024. He has never acknowledged he lost that election, and in a primetime speech last Thursday , he announced his administration would declassify documents he alleged show Chinese interference in the 2020 race. “Our elections were left vulnerable to being rigged and stolen, and the trust of the American people was lost,” Trump said Thursday. “This cannot be allowed to continue.”
sources.headline: The top of Arizona’s GOP ticket is littered with 2020 election deniers

sources.body: Trump-endorsed Rep. Andy Biggs has won the GOP primary to face Democratic Gov. Katie Hobbs in November, setting up a marquee governor’s race in one of America’s most competitive states. Biggs, a former chair of the hardline House Freedom Caucus and a close ally of President Donald Trump, easily dealt with his only serious challenger, Rep. David Schweikert, on Tuesday. Before the primary results were official, he had already begun pivoting to the general election against Hobbs, who is seen as one of the most vulnerable Democratic governors this fall. Republicans feel bullish about their odds of flipping the governorship, after Trump carried the state by five points in 2024. But even though Trump won last time, the state has gradually shifted to pure tossup territory over the past few years, electing Democrats to both Senate seats, as well as the attorney general and secretary of state’s offices. Hobbs had already shifted her focus to Biggs as well. Her campaign has amassed a significant warchest for the race and has already started running ads. Biggs, given his lack of a significant primary challenge, has been able to hold onto more cash than his GOP rivals, but is still behind Hobbs. The race will test whether Republicans, who are starting down a potentially bruising midterm cycle, can reclaim the top office of a state that has long been a place of success for the party. During the Republican primary debate, Biggs spent more time talking about his working relationship with House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries and Arizona Democratic Sen. Mark Kelly than he did flexing his endorsement from Trump or highlighting his House record, including his time atop the Freedom Caucus. Instead, he tried to refocus on his bipartisanship in Congress and his leadership in the state legislature. “I know how to reach across the aisle,” Biggs said during the debate. But he will still have to contend with many of his past stances, including his role in trying to overturn the 2020 election . And Hobbs is already working to ensure voters know where Biggs stands on key issues as a longtime member of the House, including his anti-abortion positions and his role in 2020. Her campaign has repeatedly attacked him as “too extreme” for the state and has dubbed him “basement Biggs” for keeping a relatively low profile throughout the GOP primary.
sources.headline: Rep. Andy Biggs wins GOP primary for Arizona governor

sources.body: A scandal-plagued House candidate powered by President Donald Trump’s endorsement won a messy primary Tuesday. Mark Lamb, the former Pinal County sheriff, overcame a late push from self-funding candidate Daniel Keenan in Arizona’s 5th District. Lamb will enter November as the heavy frontrunner in the deep-red Phoenix-area district to replace Rep. Andy Biggs (R-Ariz.), who is running for governor. Keenan had poured nearly $2 million of his own cash into the campaign and attempted to make Lamb’s alleged sexual misconduct and abuse of power central to the race. Lamb has denied the allegations. But Lamb — bolstered by Trump and Arizona-based Turning Point USA — managed to ward off the attacks. The race’s outcome reveals the strength of Trump’s endorsement, and it’s the latest example of how willing GOP primary voters have been to accept scandal-ridden candidates favored by the president. Lamb, who served as sheriff until 2024, earned national notoriety as a gun-toting critic of the Biden administration’s immigration and border policy. He secured Trump’s backing for the 5th District contest last year. Then in May, the Arizona Republic launched a multi-part investigation alleging that Lamb engaged in a yearslong pattern of sending sexual images and instigating intimate encounters between him, his wife and others. One woman involved, Jillian Stannard, accused Lamb of encouraging her husband to have sex with other women, including Lamb’s wife. She also alleged that Lamb threatened her, saying there would be consequences, when Stannard raised concerns to religious leaders. Screenshots the Republic obtained of messages with another woman, Tammy Peacock, suggest Lamb — then a sitting county sheriff — threatened to leverage Arizona’s revenge porn law against her. In a statement last week, Lamb spokesperson Ed Morabito told POLITICO that “these allegations are false and come from long-discredited political opponents.” Lamb did not lose any significant endorsements in the wake of the scandals — Trump reinforced his endorsement in mid-July — even as Keenan made the allegations central to his campaign.
sources.headline: Mark Lamb wins Arizona House primary despite sex scandals

sources.body: Former Arizona Cardinals kicker Jay Feely cruised to victory in Arizona’s 1st District on Tuesday, one of the nation’s most competitive battlegrounds. Feely, a close ally of President Donald Trump, leveraged Trump’s endorsement to topple former state Rep. Joseph Chaplik. He’ll face a steep challenge defeating the Democratic nominee in November: The 1st District, which spans the affluent suburbs north of Phoenix, is one of House Democrats’ top targets after Trump won it by just three points in 2024. Feely is looking to keep the seat under GOP control amid a midterm year that presents major headwinds for the party. During the primary, Chaplik, a member of the Arizona Freedom Caucus, attacked Feely for being insufficiently conservative on gun rights and for his humanitarian work in Haiti . But Feely, one of Trump’s occasional golf partners, leaned on his proximity to the president. “Who can call [Trump] and who can get him to answer? That’s me,” Feely said in a debate. “He will take my call and he will listen. I have the ability to have the ear of the president.” Feely, a former professional football player who counts Tom Brady among his college roommates, first announced his candidacy for GOP Rep. Andy Biggs’ seat in the 5th District. But after Trump backed former Pinal County sheriff Mark Lamb in that race, Feely switched to the 1st District, where he earned the president’s dual endorsement, alongside former state GOP chair Gina Swoboda. "Both fierce advocates of Make America Great Again, Jay and Gina have been with us from the very beginning, and either one would be an incredible Representative," Trump said in January on Truth Social . In February, Swoboda dropped her candidacy for the 1st District and instead ran for secretary of state, leaving the path clear for Feely. As a member of the National Republican Congressional Committee’s MAGA Majority fundraising program, Feely will likely be a top recipient of outside Republican spending in the months leading to November.
sources.headline: Trump-backed Jay Feely wins GOP primary in Arizona’s battleground 1st District

stories.headline: House narrowly passes stopgap spending bill as shutdown fight looms
South Carolina special election becomes latest test of Trump’s sway

sources.body: The race to fill Lindsey Graham’s seat has created a new test of President Donald Trump’s power. South Carolina Republicans aren’t sure Trump will pass. Sen. Darline Graham, who was appointed last week to fill the remainder of her late brother's term, announced Monday she will seek a full term for her brother’s old seat. Her decision came after winning Trump’s backing, and his endorsement of the political newcomer had the potential to scare away challengers. But other Republicans are still charging forward, seeing an opening in a race with a highly untested candidate. Their determination is a signal that challenging a Trump-backed candidate may no longer be the career-ender it once was within the party. “Even folks that didn’t like Senator [Lindsey] Graham or didn’t vote for him in the primary were pretty happy about the appointment, as a tribute to Senator Graham,” said CJ Westfall, chair of the Dorchester County GOP. “But once we saw the president jump in and support her, 95 percent are saying, ‘What the hell are we doing here? We don’t know how she votes. We don’t know what she believes.’” “People that were excited about the appointment are now skeptical of the endorsement,” Westfall said. Rep. Russell Fry, one of Trump’s most loyal congressional allies, announced earlier Monday he was jumping in. He joins Rep. Ralph Norman, who declared his own candidacy over the weekend , along with self-funding businessman Mark Lynch, who lost to the former senator in the primary. A number of other Republicans are mulling runs as well, including former Gov. Mark Sanford and Lt. Gov. Pam Evette, who lost her own bid for governor a few weeks ago in spite of Trump’s endorsement. While Trump’s endorsees have largely won their primaries, Evette’s recent loss isn’t his only setback. His picks lost in the Iowa and Georgia gubernatorial races. "I think people saw [Trump's] endorsement of Pam Evette and her terrible performance and know this race could be up for grabs like the gubernatorial was,” said a Republican operative unaffiliated with the race who was granted anonymity to candidly discuss the landscape. While Graham’s candidacy will test the power of Trump’s direct endorsement, there’s no question the MAGA movement is still the dominant force in South Carolina politics: All Republicans in the race are running as unapologetic supporters of the president, competing among themselves to prove who is the most authentic champion of Trump’s agenda — a dynamic that has defined GOP primaries across the country this year. Norman told reporters on Monday that Trump’s endorsement “helps a great deal.” “That's why I ask[ed] him for it,” he added. “I'd rather have it, but it's up to the people." In Fry’s launch video , he presented himself as a Trump-aligned “MAGA warrior” — and included pictures of him grinning alongside the president. In a statement, Fry campaign spokesperson Luke Byars noted Fry has “been honored to receive President Trump’s endorsement” in his previous elections. “Our focus for the next three weeks remains ensuring South Carolina has a conservative fighter in the United States Senate who will advance the President’s agenda and deliver real results for South Carolina families,” Byars said. Some Republicans said Graham’s lack of prior elected experience and the original expectation that she would only serve the remainder of her brother’s term have kept the field open. "There is a desire to avoid yet another bruising Republican primary, right after we had a pretty contentious one for governor," said Joel Sawyer, a longtime GOP strategist in South Carolina who’s not involved in the race. "But I don't think that's going to be the case." Trump’s endorsement of Graham gives her instant credibility despite her lack of electoral experience. But her campaign announcement on Monday was met with surprise and confusion by many across the state. Most saw her appointment as a respectful gesture to the Graham family, and she was expected to be a caretaker who would hold the seat until the end of her brother’s term in January. Her interest in running for a full term even caught South Carolina party brass off-guard: Drew McKissick, chair of the South Carolina Republican Party, told POLITICO Friday he thought Graham told Gov. Henry McMaster she’d “accept the appointment to fill the rest of the term, not to be a candidate for it.” A South Carolina GOP rule adopted earlier this year requires candidates for office to have voted in at least two of the past three statewide GOP primaries. According to local political outlet FITSNews , Graham voted in the 2024 GOP runoff but neither the 2022 nor 2024 GOP primaries. Whether Graham qualifies depends on whether, alongside her vote last month, her 2024 runoff vote counts as a primary vote under the new rule. The Dorchester County Republican Party announced it was formally requesting the state party’s rules committee review Graham’s eligibility. McKissick, the state party chair, said Monday he hadn’t taken a look at the issue yet since the filing window doesn’t open until midday Tuesday, so no one had officially filed to run. “As a result I haven’t seen information from any candidates yet,” he told POLITICO. “But suffice it to say that we will look at all of the various candidates’ information and official voting records from the state election commission when they do.” A spokesperson for Graham’s campaign said she meets the state GOP’s primary voting requirement to file as a Senate candidate and is “qualified to run because she has real experience leading.” “As to why she is running, it's quite simple: she is a strong conservative who wants to build upon her brother's legacy. There were never any conversations surrounding her appointment that were contingent upon serving only the remainder of Senator Lindsey Graham's term,” Abby Zilch, a spokesperson for Graham’s campaign, said in a statement. Trump’s endorsement power has been decisive across contested Republican primaries over the last several months, repeatedly propelling his favored candidate across the finish line. But his track record in South Carolina is murkier. After backing Evette early in the gubernatorial race only to see her struggle in the runoff, Trump offered a last-minute dual-endorsement to also back Attorney General Alan Wilson. Wilson ultimately beat Evette in the runoff by more than 30 points. “I think it’s a nice sentiment,” said another GOP strategist of Trump’s endorsement granted anonymity to speak openly. “But at the end of the day she has no record, nor a right to the seat all because her brother held it. This is up to the people of South Carolina to decide.” On Capitol Hill, Senate Majority Leader John Thune said he would stay out of the August 11 special election. Thune, who was close friends with Lindsey Graham, called his sister a “strong candidate” but said “I’ll let voters in South Carolina decide it.” Other senators are diving in. Sens. Mike Lee (R-Utah) and Rick Scott (R-Florida), staunch allies of the president, announced their support for Norman over the weekend, publicly bucking Trump to back one of the House’s most conservative members. Both Lee and Scott specifically cited Norman’s support for the Save America Act, the election reform bill that has been a central focus for Trump in recent months. Norman also earned the nod of Turning Point Action. Meanwhile, Norman — who passed on another term in the House to make an unsuccessful run for governor — has suggested Fry, his House colleague, could jeopardize Republicans’ House majority. If Fry wins the special election for Graham’s seat, the state would have to call a special election to fill his House seat, potentially leaving it empty for months. “I respect Russell Fry and I have enjoyed serving with him in the House, but the reality is, his candidacy could leave his seat vacant until May 18th,” Norman wrote Monday . GOP Rep. Nancy Mace announced Monday she would not enter the race. The candidate filing window closes on July 28. Election Day is just two weeks later. The breakneck speed could play to Graham’s favor: She has the benefit of her brother’s campaign apparatus, which mobilized to win a primary outright last month while many of the state’s other contested races went to runoffs. And the memorial services for Lindsey Graham — scheduled for July 28 in Washington and July 29 in South Carolina — will shrink the calendar further. “We say it's a three-week campaign, but really it's a two-week campaign after the funeral,” said Tyson Grinstead, an RNC committeeman and Richland County GOP chair. “I'm sure there'll be ads and stuff, but it's going to be hard for everybody to focus on campaign rallies and make a decision on this, in terms of voters, until after the funeral.” If no candidate reaches 50% of the vote, a runoff election would be held in late August. “We don't always understand what's going on in D.C.,” said Jeff Davis, the GOP chair in Greenville County. “We try to give the president a lot of grace on these things. Would I have picked, you know, another Graham to replace Lindsey Graham? Probably not.” “We don't know her yet,” Davis added. “But we're open and trusting in what the president and his team are doing.” Katherine Tully-McManus contributed to this report. CORRECTION: An earlier version of this report gave the wrong surname for Greenville County GOP chair Jeff Davis.
sources.headline: There’s a power struggle brewing in South Carolina, and Trump might not win

sources.body: Former Rep. Trey Gowdy insists he’s not interested in running in the South Carolina special election. But a new poll suggests Gowdy would be formidable if he changed his mind. Gowdy holds a massive lead over seven other potential and declared GOP candidates for the late Sen. Lindsey Graham’s seat, according to a poll from Republican-aligned firm Peak Insights that was obtained by POLITICO . Gowdy draws 38 percent of the vote. The actual candidates in the race are bunched closely, with no clear frontrunner: Rep. Ralph Norman trails at 13 percent support, followed closely by both Rep. Russell Fry and Sen. Darline Graham, Lindsey’s sister who is running for a full term after being appointed, who both sit at 9 percent. Businessman Mark Lynch, a self-funder who lost a Senate primary to Graham last summer, pulls 7 percent support. The survey of 900 likely GOP primary voters for South Carolina’s special election was conducted before anyone formally entered the contest. The election will be a test of Trump’s power. He endorsed Darline Graham last week, but that hasn’t stopped other prominent South Carolina Republicans from jumping in the race, all of them touting their loyalty to the president — despite not having his formal support. And at least according to the poll, his endorsement doesn’t shift things much. When respondents are informed that Trump “asked Darline Graham Nordone to run in the special election for U.S. Senate, saying if she runs, she has his complete and total endorsement,” Graham’s support improves from 9 percent to 16 percent, and Gowdy falls from 38 percent to 35 percent. The poll is a sign of why some prominent Republicans — like his longtime friend, Sen. Tim Scott — may want Gowdy in the race. Peak Insights has polled for Scott, who chairs the National Republican Senatorial Committee, as well as the NRSC and the Republican National Committee among many others. Gowdy left the U.S. House in 2019 and now hosts a show on Fox News. Through a spokesperson, he said he “has not had any conversations about the open senate seat and does not have any interest in it.” That hasn’t stopped Scott from pushing his longtime buddy to run. Scott advocated for Gowdy to be appointed to the vacant Senate seat after Lindsey Graham’s death, before South Carolina Gov. Henry McMaster appointed Darline Graham to the seat. Scott announced last week he was recruiting Gowdy to run in the special election, before Graham announced she would run. “I can't think of a better person that I think best represents the heart of South Carolina than Trey Gowdy,” said Scott during a press conference Wednesday. “I'm trying to get him interested in it, but we'll see if that works out at all.” Scott and the NRSC have not yet issued an endorsement in the special election. Scott praised Graham during a Fox News interview Monday, saying she is “strong so far, calm under pressure, and really poised.”
sources.headline: South Carolina Senate poll shows surprising frontrunner: Trey Gowdy

sources.headline: Beaches closed and sandbags filled as Tropical Storm Bertha approaches the Gulf Coast

sources.body:
Copyright 2026 The Associated Press. All Rights Reserved.

Copyright 2026 The Associated Press. All Rights Reserved.

This satellite image from the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration taken at 7:31pm EDT shows Tropical Storm Bertha in the Gulf of Mexico on Monday, July 21, 2026. (NOAA via AP)

NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Tropical Storm Bertha formed Monday evening in the Gulf of Mexico south of the Florida Panhandle as it drifts erratically toward populated coastal areas bearing a threat of heavy rain, storm surge and damaging wind gusts, forecasters say.

Meanwhile, in the eastern Pacific Ocean, Hurricane Fausto formed far from Mexico’s Baja California peninsula and posed no threat to land Monday night.

The center of Bertha was located about 140 miles (225 km) south of Panama City, Florida, in the northern Gulf. Maximum sustained winds were around 40 mph (65 kph) and some additional strengthening is expected as the storm approaches land, according to the Miami-based National Hurricane Center.

The slow-moving storm drifted for hours on Monday, but is expected to stay near or along the northern Gulf Coast early this week, unleashing heavy rain on coastal areas of Florida, Alabama, Mississippi and Louisiana as it pushes west toward Texas.

Bertha is the second tropical storm of the Atlantic hurricane season . Tropical Storm Arthur last month brought heavy rainfall to the southeastern United States.

The storm was nearly stationary by Monday night, but it was expected to make a west-northwestward or westward turn toward the coast over the next few days, according to the hurricane center.

A tropical storm warning is in effect for a wide area from the Alabama-Florida border westward to southeastern Louisiana at the Jefferson and Plaquemines parish line. A storm surge watch also has been posted from the mouth of the Mississippi River to the Alabama-Florida border on the Gulf Coast.

Potentially life-threatening storm surges of up to four feet (1.2 meters) could occur in portions of the Louisiana, Alabama and Florida, the hurricane center said, warning Bertha could spin off an isolated tornado or two over the Florida Big Bend region in the week.

Areas of flash flooding will also be possible through Friday along the coast from western Florida to middle Texas, forecasters said. They added that rainfall could total 3 to 5 inches (7 to 12 centimeters) along the Louisiana coastline with higher amounts in some scattered areas.

Red flags were flying on the beach at Orange Beach, Alabama, cautioning swimmers about high surf and currents. In Escambia County, Florida, emergency officials offered sand for people living in flood-prone areas to use for sandbags.

Fausto gained hurricane status Monday night and was about 745 miles (1,200 km) southwest of the southern tip of the Baja California peninsula, forecasters said.

The U.S. National Hurricane Center in Miami said Fausto had top sustained winds of 75 mph (120 kph) and was moving to the west-northwest at 9 mph 15 (kph).

No coastal watches or warnings were in effect though ocean swells generated by the storm could pummel the Baja California coast and cause life-threatening surf and rip tide conditions, the center said.
sources.headline: Tropical Storm Bertha drifts toward land as Hurricane Fausto forms in the eastern Pacific

stories.headline: Puigdemont asks European Commission to step in over Spain’s amnesty delays

sources.body:
La defensa del expresidente catalán Carles Puigdemont ha presentado una denuncia contra España ante la Comisión Europea por "incumplimiento" del derecho europeo. Considera que el Tribunal de Cuentas está obstruyendo la aplicación de la ley de amnistía tras la sentencia del Tribunal de Justicia de la Unión Europea (TJUE) que la avaló.

El escrito elaborado por el abogado de Puigdemont, Gonzalo Boye, avanzado por VilaWeb y al que ha tenido acceso RTVE, responde a una providencia emitida el lunes, en la que el tribunal fiscalizador del Estado preguntó a acusaciones y defensas si cabe la "posibilidad" de no amnistiar a la treintena de ex altos cargos del Govern encausados por los gastos del 'procés', entre ellos Puigdemont, si se utilizaron fondos de la Unión Europea para sufragarlos.

"En más de cuatro años de procedimiento, con la instrucción agotada y las actuaciones conclusas para sentencia, nadie —ni las demandantes ni el propio órgano— sostuvo nunca que se hubieran empleado fondos de la Unión, y la providencia no identifica ningún elemento sobrevenido", se puede leer en el documento, que tilda la actuación del Tribunal de Cuentas de "estrategia obstruccionista".

La "denuncia" contra el Reino de España por "incumplimiento del derecho de la Unión Europea", fechada este martes y dirigida a la secretaría general de la Comisión Europea en nombre de Puigdemont, el eurodiputado Toni Comín y el exconseller Lluís Puig , pide abrir contra España "el procedimiento de infracción previsto" en el artículo 258 del Tratado de Funcionamiento de la UE, por el "incumplimiento" de la sentencia del TJUE que avala la amnistía .

El escrito dice que el Tribunal de Cuentas "difundió una nota de prensa oficial que tergiversa el sentido del fallo" del TJUE, al afirmar por ejemplo que este fallo "admite las ocho cuestiones planteadas" por el tribunal español, pese a que varias de ellas "fueron declaradas inadmisibles".

Asimismo, ha dirigido un escrito de alegaciones al Tribunal de Cuentas en el que niega que los gastos del 'procés' se sufragaran con "fondos procedentes del presupuesto de la Unión" y reclama que "dicte, dentro del plazo máximo de dos meses , una resolución que declare amnistiados los hechos".

stories.headline: Guadalajara megafire scorches 30,000 hectares as Sánchez visits disaster zone

sources.body:
España vive este miércoles una de las jornadas más complejas de la década en la lucha contra el fuego, marcada por la visita institucional del presidente del Gobierno, Pedro Sánchez, y el presidente de la Junta de Castilla-La Mancha, Emiliano García-Page, a la zona cero del incendio de la Sierra Norte de Guadalajara . La visita está anunciada a las 11:00 de la mañana en la agenda de Moncloa sin rueda de prensa posterior y con una declaración institucional en su lugar.

Sánchez tiene previsto visitar las zonas afectadas por el incendio de La Mierla y mantener un encuentro con los responsables de los dispositivos de emergencias y de coordinación que trabajan en la extinción en el Puesto de Mando Avanzado de Tamajón , Guadalajara.

El fuego de La Mierla, que continúa activo , se ha consolidado ya como el segundo más grave de la serie histórica del país por superficie calcinada, con una estimación de entre 30.000 y 32.000 hectáreas afectadas.

El balance nacional es desolador ya que en lo que va de año han ardido 90.000 hectáreas , una cifra que cuadruplica los datos registrados en el mismo periodo de 2025.

La situación en la provincia de Guadalajara acapara la atención nacional. El incendio forestal de La Mierla , iniciado el pasado 16 de julio por causas que apuntan a labores agrícolas , ha superado ya todas las previsiones.

Con más de 30.000 hectáreas arrasadas, este siniestro solo es superado en la historia de España por el incendio de Larouco (Ourense, Lugo y León) de 2025, que quemó 37.765 hectáreas. Por debajo queda ya el histórico incendio de Minas de Riotinto de 2004, que afectó a 29.867 hectáreas en Huelva.

Este fuego se ha convertido además en el mayor desastre forestal de la historia de Castilla-La Mancha , superando con creces las 13.000 hectáreas que ardieron en Riba de Saelices en el año 2005.

La vicepresidenta tercera y ministra para la Transición Ecológica, Sara Aagesen, ha confirmado que el Ejecutivo declarará la zona como "gravemente afectada por una emergencia de Protección Civil" , lo que permitirá que el Gobierno central sufrague el 50% del coste de la recuperación de la Sierra Norte.

A pesar de la magnitud de la tragedia, el director de la emergencia, Juan José Fernández, ha aportado una nota de optimismo al señalar que las operaciones progresan "de manera positiva". Aunque el perímetro sigue creciendo, lo hace a una velocidad mucho menor que en los días anteriores.

No obstante, la situación en la provincia se mantiene tensa debido al incendio de Selas , que ha obligado a evacuar preventivamente a los vecinos de Establés, Concha, Torrubia, Tartanedo, Pardos y Aragoncillo para evitar que las llamas crucen a la provincia de Soria .

En Castilla y León , la jornada de este miércoles comienza con mejores noticias para parte de la población desalojada. El Centro de Coordinación Operativa Integrado (Cecopi) de Segovia ha autorizado el regreso a sus domicilios de los vecinos de seis localidades: Tenzuela, Losana de Pirón, La Cuesta, Aldeasaz, Caballar y Santo Domingo de Pirón. Estos vecinos habían sido evacuados debido al incendio iniciado este lunes en la zona de Brieva .
sources.headline: S�nchez se desplaza al incendio de Guadalajara en un a�o cr�tico con m�s de 90.000 hect�reas calcinadas en Espa�a

sources.body:
El incendio de La Mierla (Guadalajara), que ya es el más grande registrado en Castilla-La Mancha al haber ardido al menos 30.000 hectáreas, ha afectado a aproximadamente una cuarta parte del Parque Natural de la Sierra Norte. Este espacio protegido es una de las joyas de la naturaleza castellano-manchega, enclave de valiosos bosques y hábitat del lobo. Declarada parque natural en 2011, esta sierra ocupa 116.953 hectáreas en el noroeste de la provincia de Guadalajara a lo largo de 35 términos municipales.

Una primera aproximación a cargo de expertos de Ecologistas en Acción en Guadalajara resalta la fuerte afectación del incendio sobre este espacio protegido, en el que se habrían causado daños muy graves en los bosques de ribera de los ríos Sorbe, Bornova y Cañamares, así como en una zona de repoblación de pinares (de valor menor).

“Hablamos de bosques de ribera de gran biodiversidad y con especies protegidas, como el aliso”, señala Alberto Mayor, portavoz de esta organización conservacionista en Guadalajara. El lugar es también hábitat del lobo y del águila perdicera. También habría sido afectada la reserva fluvial del río Pelagañinas.

El gobierno castellano-manchego no ha entrado aún en hacer ninguna valoración sobre el impacto ecológico producido por el siniestro.

“El daño ecológico que se está produciendo es, de momento, incalculable; se hará una valoración una vez se conozca el alcance real de los daños”, añade, en cambio, Mayor.

La ministra para la Transición Ecológica, Sara Aagesen, ha asegurado que el Gobierno central “va a estar” sufragará “el 50% del coste de la recuperación de una zona de tanto valor como es esta Sierra Norte”. “Es clave ayudando en esta extinción, pero también va a estar después en esa recuperación en territorio”, aseguró.

El incendio forestal ha provocado la evacuación de cerca de 1.300 personas de 34 núcleos habitados o aldeas y el confinamiento de otros 14. La lucha contra las llamas están resultando muy complicadas porque se originó en una zona prácticamente deshabitada, con abundante vegetación, lo que facilitó su rápida propagación. Además, el viento ha sido muy desfavorable.

Cerca de 400 efectivos de la Unidad Militar de Emergencias (UME), Infocam, la Comunidad de Madrid y el Ministerio para la Transición Ecológica trabajan en la zona junto con 72 medios terrestres y una treintena de aviones y helicópteros. Junto a ellos, agentes de la Guardia Civil colaboran en las tareas de evacuación de los vecinos y en garantizar la seguridad en las carreteras.

La noticia más positiva es que los bomberos han logrado “prácticamente detener” el avance del fuego hacia la ciudad de Soria (40.000 habitantes), en la vecina región de Castilla y León. El presidente del Gobierno, Pedro Sánchez, visita este miércoles las zonas afectadas.

Ecologistas en Acción exige a las administraciones “medios adecuados contra los incendios de sexta generación y el cambio climático”. Esta organización lamenta profundamente la devastación que está provocando el siniestro. Agradece el esfuerzo que están haciendo los medios de lucha contra los incendios, pero exige que “se adecúen a la nueva realidad marcada por el cambio climático y los incendios de sexta generación, dado que los hechos están demostrando que no son suficientes, a pesar de la colaboración de distintas administraciones para intentar controlarlo”.
sources.headline: Arde el parque natural de la Sierra Norte, enclave de valiosos bosques y hábitat del lobo

stories.headline: Judicial watchdog declines to punish gender-violence judge who said women have “advantages”

sources.body: <p>La autoridad disciplinaria del Consejo General del Poder Judicial (CGPJ) rechaza expedientar al juez de violencia de género David Maman, instructor de <a href="https://elpais.com/espana/2026-02-17/admitida-a-tramite-una-querella-por-agresion-sexual-contra-el-jefe-de-la-policia-nacional.html" rel="" title="https://elpais.com/espana/2026-02-17/admitida-a-tramite-una-querella-por-agresion-sexual-contra-el-jefe-de-la-policia-nacional.html">la investigación contra el exjefe de la Policía José Ángel González</a>, por afirmar que <a href="https://elpais.com/espana/2026-04-23/el-juez-del-caso-de-violacion-contra-el-exjefe-de-la-policia-critica-las-ventajas-que-las-mujeres-tienen-para-denunciar.html" rel="" title="https://elpais.com/espana/2026-04-23/el-juez-del-caso-de-violacion-contra-el-exjefe-de-la-policia-critica-las-ventajas-que-las-mujeres-tienen-para-denunciar.html">las mujeres tienen “ventajas” para denunciar</a> a sus parejas o exparejas. El llamado promotor de la acción disciplinaria, Ricardo Conde, abrió una investigación al juez en abril, después de que el Ministerio de Igualdad presentara una queja ante el consejo por las afirmaciones de Maman, que Igualdad considera “incompatibles” con la “imparcialidad” judicial. Tres meses después, Conde ha propuesto archivar las pesquisas al considerar que las palabras del magistrado no son sancionables, según señalan fuentes del órgano de gobierno de los jueces. La comisión permanente tenía que decidir este martes si cerraban el caso o instaban a Conde a seguir adelante con la investigación, pero ha aplazado la decisión ante la falta de acuerdo.</p><p><a href="https://elpais.com/espana/2026-07-21/la-autoridad-disciplinaria-del-poder-judicial-rechaza-expedientar-al-juez-de-violencia-de-genero-que-dijo-que-las-mujeres-tienen-ventajas-para-denunciar.html" target="_blank">Seguir leyendo</a></p>
sources.headline: La autoridad disciplinaria del Poder Judicial rechaza expedientar al juez de violencia de género que dijo que las mujeres tienen “ventajas” para denunciar

stories.headline: Barcelona studies fund to buy iconic shops as rent regulation push grows

sources.body: (ACN) L’alcalde de Barcelona, Jaume Collboni , creu que cal obrir un debat sobre el dret al tanteig comercial perquè la Generalitat o l’Ajuntament de Barcelona tinguin opció de compra preferent quan es ven el local d’un comerç que, per la seva història, és emblemàtic o es considera que s’ha de preservar. En una entrevista a l’ACN ha dit que es tractaria d’utilitzar el dret de tanteig i retracte per comprar locals comercials actius emblemàtics per preservar-los i que sigui l’Administració qui faci de gestor del patrimoni. Això permetria ajudar negocis en actiu que es troben amb ofertes de grans cadenes per quedar-se amb el local o els dupliquen o tripliquen el preu del lloguer per expulsar-los. També et pot interessar Barcelona planteja un pla per protegir els comerços emblemàtics Marc Bermejo Roger Torres i Liñán 2 minuts de lectura Converses amb la Generalitat aquest 2026 “Crec que estem en disposició d’obrir un debat amb la Generalitat abans que s’acabi aquest any i comencem a estudiar quines mesures de caràcter legislatiu i econòmic i pressupostari s’haurien d’adoptar per crear aquest fons de compra estratègica de locals comercials que estiguin en situació de risc i que tinguin interès patrimonial, identitari o d’activitat claus per molts barris de la ciutat de Barcelona o d’altres ciutats”, afirma. Collboni considera que els ajuntaments són els qui han de tenir l’última paraula i posa damunt la taula fer-ho especialment amb els eixos comercials estratègics i zones tensionades turísticament , els anomenats espais de gran afluència (EGA), que és on hi ha “més pressió immobiliària especulativa que expulsa i gentrifica el comerç tradicional”. També et pot interessar Comerços de Barcelona apel·len a la solidaritat dels propietaris pel lloguer dels locals Ester Magdaleno 3 minuts de lectura La regulació dels lloguers comercials, al plenari En paral·lel, el PSC portarà al plenari d’aquest divendres una proposició per regular els lloguers de comerços de barri o emblemàtics per garantir-ne la continuïtat i evitar que es perdin per pujades de preu a les quals no poden fer front. En la proposta s’insta el Govern espanyol a impulsar una modificació de la LAU perquè incorpori instruments específics per “regular o moderar els increments dels lloguers”. Alhora, també insta el Congrés a habilitar mecanismes que permetin establir mesures de contenció dels preus en algunes zones. També et pot interessar Barcelona estudiarà vies per regular els lloguers d’establiments comercials en zones tensionades Redacció 1 minut de lectura Collboni apunta que els plans d’usos que ha adoptat Barcelona han contribuït a un equilibri en l’oferta comercial, però no han evitat la pèrdua de molts locals i comerços . Per això, creu que la regulació dels lloguers seria una via per ajudar els locals que han de fer front a increments de preus quan hi ha un canvi de propietat i un contracte nou. L’alcalde defensa que “hi ha recorregut” per prendre més mesures que protegeixin el comerç local de proximitat i adverteix que no fer res pot conduir a la “desertificació comercial”.
sources.headline: Collboni planteja crear un fons per comprar locals comercials emblemàtics en risc

sources.body:
ERC reivindica com a seva la proposta que el govern de Jaume Collboni vol impulsar ara per regular el lloguer dels locals comercials. En el plenari de divendres, el darrer del curs polític, el PSC proposarà instar al govern espanyol i al Congrés dels Diputats a fer els canvis necessaris per “regular o moderar els increments dels lloguers comercials”. En una storie a Instagram la presidenta d’ERC a l’Ajuntament i alcaldable, Elisenda Alamany , diu que fa tres anys que ells defensen aquesta necessitat perquè hi ha molts locals a la ciutat, alguns “de tota la vida”, que han hagut de tancar perquè els titulars no poden assumir el cost del lloguer. Alamany també critica que la iniciativa arriba a menys d’un any de les eleccions municipals i denuncia que els socialistes no hagin plantejat res sobre aquesta qüestió a l’Ajuntament, l’Estat i la Generalitat, les tres administracions que governen, des de fa tres i dos anys, respectivament.

La proposició que el PSC presentarà divendres manifesta el compromís de l’Ajuntament en la defensa dels comerç de proximitat i dels establiments singulars i tradicionals com a elements fonamentals de la identitat dels barris i de la ciutat, i planteja impulsar una reforma de la LAU (Llei d’Arrendaments Urbans) per establir mesures que permetin limitar o contenir els preus dels locals comercials. El PSC de Collboni vols que es faci “en aquelles zones especialment afectades” per l’increment de preus, amb l’objectiu de garantir la permanència dels petits comerciants, preservar la diversitat comercial dels barris i evitar la substitució del comerç de proximitat.

Fonts d’ERC expliquen al TOT Barcelona que al llarg del mandat municipal han portat moltes iniciatives en aquesta línia i sempre la resposta ha estat “que era molt difícil, que no es podia fer”. Arran dels molts tancaments que hi ha a la ciutat, amb el lloguer com a raó principal, es van reunir amb els eixos comercials i aquests van pressionar per intentar desencallar la situació. Això hauria fet que el govern preguntés per aquesta qüestió concreta en el darrer Baròmetre municipal . El suport a una regulació del lloguer dels locals comercials va aconseguir un suport del 76% per part dels enquestats.

Entre els diferents locals que han hagut de plegar a Barcelona aquest any per increments del lloguer hi ha la ferreteria Camps i el forn Santa Clara , ambdós a Gràcia. En el cas de la ferreteria, la propietat volia un lloguer mensual de 15.000 euros, segons van explicar els titulars del negoci.
sources.headline: ERC reivindica com a seva la intenció de Collboni de regular els lloguers comercials

stories.headline: Fire at Turó de la Rovira renews calls to curb nightly parties at Barcelona overlook

sources.body: La nit de dilluns a dimarts un incendi a tocar de les bateries antiaèries del turó de la Rovira ha alertat els veïns del carrer de Maria Labèrnia. Els veïns relacionen el foc amb el llançament de petards i focs artificials per part de persones que feien botellots dins del recinte de les bateries, tot i estar tancat per les nits. Dues dotacions de bombers han extingit les flames, que han cremat 100 m² de matolls baixos als peus de les bateries. Demanen l’actuació de la Urbana Per als veïns, l’incendi és la gota que fa vessar el vas, perquè arriba només 15 dies després de la creació d’un grup a l’Ajuntament per frenar l’incivisme a la zona . El Consell Veïnal del Turó de la Rovira fa temps que denuncia els botellots i les intrusions nocturnes a la zona tancada de les bateries, i també en alguns habitatges propers, i critica que el districte d’Horta-Guinardó es va comprometre a posar càmeres de seguretat a partir del setembre, tot i que l’estiu és un dels moments més crítics de l’any en un dels punts més visitats de Barcelona. En un comunicat, demanen que la Guàrdia Urbana requisi alcohol als qui fan botellots i prengui mesures sancionadores, i que s’intensifiqui el control i sanció d’accés al recinte tancat fora d’horari. A més, reclamen la instal·lació de sanitaris portàtils, posar-hi més papereres i reforestar les zones on es fa botellot, amb aspersors d’aigua perquè no s’hi aplegui la gent a la nit. “Una zona d’impunitat absoluta” El veïnat denuncia que “s’han creat zones d’impunitat absoluta destinades a l’ oci turístic i també local, de botellot i de fer el salvatge”. El veí que va gravar les imatges ha relatat que poc abans de la mitjanit, després de sentir petards, “ens ha arribat l’olor de fum i el soroll del foc cremant branques al costat de casa”. L’Alexander ha trucat de seguida als bombers quan ha vist el foc. Assegura que viu amb tensió i por perquè en les darreres setmanes han viscut un intent d’agressió a un veí i les destrosses del seu immoble ( l’estiu del 2023 van apallissar un altre veí ), l’atropellament d’un treballador que regulava els accessos als carrers dels voltants, i el darrer ha estat l’incendi d’aquesta nit. Dos lavabos portàtils, a l’agost El districte assegura que entén el malestar veïnal i que fa mesos que treballa amb els serveis municipals i el veïnat per reforçar la gestió d’aquest espai i reduir-ne les afectacions. D’entrada durant el mes d’agost s’instal·laran dos lavabos portàtils , un al carrer Mühlberg i un al carrer Labèrnia, de divendres a dilluns , coincidint amb els dies de més afluència. Se sumaran als lavabos fixos ja existents dins del recinte arqueològic, tindran servei de neteja diària i funcionaran com a prova pilot, que s’avaluarà al setembre. Pel que fa a les obres de millora de la tanca perimetral , ja estan adjudicades i s’executaran durant aquest any. El calendari d’inici s’ha d’ajustar a les condicions de calor i de risc d’incendi, ja que es tracta d’actuacions en un entorn amb vegetació i hi pot haver risc d’espurnes. Pel que fa els dispositius de la Guàrdia Urbana, l’Ajuntament ha explicat que es revisen de manera continuada per adaptar-los a les necessitats de cada moment i a l’evolució de l’afluència de visitants. A més, durant l’agost es reforçarà la comunicació sobre les normes d’ús del turó de la Rovira per prevenir conductes incíviques que afecten la convivència, la seguretat, la conservació de l’espai i el descans veïnal, en coherència amb la campanya municipal “Poca vergonya”. També et pot interessar L’Ajuntament crea un grup per gestionar el turó de la Rovira i frenar l’incivisme a la zona Marina Segura i Vidal Ricard Cosialls 3 minuts de lectura
sources.headline: Un incendi a les bateries del turó de la Rovira atia les queixes veïnals pels botellots nocturns

stories.headline: Second stretch of Barcelona’s Ronda de Dalt ring road moves under cover

sources.body: Ja es comença a intuir la coberta del segon tram de la ronda de Dalt que es taparà per amagar els cotxes i guanyar més espai per als vianants. Una grua de grans dimensions treballa aquests dies de nit —fins al 6 d’agost— per començar a cobrir 340 metres de ronda, des de l’Institut Vall d’Hebron fins a l’avinguda de Vallcarca. Amb molta precisió, els operaris s’ajuden de la grua per encaixar sobre el nou mur que han aixecat al mig de la ronda unes grans plaques de 20 tones cada una. Aquest dimarts a la nit n’han posat vuit i d’aquí a dues setmanes la previsió és haver-ne posat 56. Les peces s’assimilen a unes bigues i un dels passos següents serà formigonar-les per tapar del tot la coberta de la ronda. Els treballs s’han de fer de nit perquè obliguen a tallar el trànsit i és el moment en què hi ha menys afluència de cotxes i, per tant, l’afectació també és menor. Els conductors s’han de desviar pels laterals de la ronda entre les sortides 5 i 6. També et pot interessar Talls de trànsit nocturns per les obres de cobriment de la ronda de Dalt Marta Casals Torres 1 minut de lectura Compromís per estendre el cobriment de la ronda La segona fase de cobriment se suma als 180 metres de ronda que ja estan tapats —a l’altura del Mercat de la Vall d’Hebron— i està previst que acabi a finals de l’any que ve. La urbanització de la superfície quedarà pendent per al 2028, tot i que, segons l’alcalde de Barcelona, Jaume Collboni, passat l’estiu ja se’n començarà a definir el disseny conjuntament amb els veïns. Collboni assenyala que a la coberta hi haurà espai per al lleure , zones d’estada i vegetació , espais que, als barris de muntanya com el de la Teixonera i Sant Genís dels Agudells, apunta, “no existien fins ara”. Un cop acabada la segona fase de cobriment, quedaran dues fases més en sentit Besòs i l’alcalde afirma: “El meu compromís és no aturar el cobriment”. Ara bé, admet que les obres quedaran subjectes a la disponibilitat pressupostària del moment i el calendari. Els veïns volen més comunicació El cobriment de la ronda de Dalt és una demanda històrica dels veïns que viuen als barris limítrofs amb aquesta via tan transitada. Veuen les obres imprescindibles per guanyar qualitat de vida i demanen celeritat. També més comunicació amb l’Ajuntament. Des de les associacions veïnals de la Teixonera i Sant Genís dels Agudells asseguren que l’última reunió amb el consistori va ser fa un any i reclamen poder tenir més detalls de l’estat de les obres . També et pot interessar Entitats i veïns celebren l’inici de la segona fase de les obres de cobertura de la ronda de Dalt Jessica Moreno Cusac 1 minut de lectura
sources.headline: Comença el cobriment del segon tram de la ronda de Dalt amb la instal·lació de plaques de formigó

sources.body:
La cobertura final del tram de la ronda de Dalt , entre l’avinguda de Vallcarca i l’IES Vall d’Hebron, ha començat aquesta setmana. En total, s’instal·laran 101 lloses, que permetran guanyar 16.000 metres quadrats d’espai ciutadà. Segons l’Ajuntament, fins al 6 d’agost se’n col·locaran 56.

Aquest nou tram de la cobertura de la ronda de Dalt ha estat un dels grans projectes urbanístics del govern de Jaume Collboni, juntament amb l’impuls definitiu de la reforma de la Rambla , entre altres. Per a l’alcalde, es fa “un salt qualitatiu molt important” , ja que era una reivindicació llargament reclamada pels veïns de la Vall d’Hebron , Montbau, la Teixonera , Sant Genís dels Agudells i Vallcarc a”. Aquesta actuació permet “recosir els barris, guanyar espai públic i verd i pacificar les rondes”.

Un cop acabi la instal·lació del centenar de prelloses, les feines es concentraran en l’interior del futur túnel, amb la il·luminació, ventiladors, sistemes d’informació i seguretat i sistemes de comunicació, i, posteriorment, s’executarà la cobertura total i definitiva del túnel. Les obres s’allargaran tot el 2027.

La col·locació de les prelloses es fa amb una grua de grans dimensions que afectarà la calçada lateral de la ronda, al passeig de la Vall d’Hebron, i obliga a desviar el trànsit en sentit Besòs, habilitant els dos sentits per la calçada del costat muntanya del passeig. També per qüestions de seguretat es tallarà de manera total el trànsit en tots dos sentits de la ronda, entre les sortides 5 (Vall d’Hebron) i 6 (Vallcarca). Els treballs es faran sempre de nit de dilluns a dijous, fins al 6 d’agost.

Un cop instal·lades les primeres 56 peces, es faran les actuacions relacionades amb el cablejat, seguretat, ventilació i enllumenat, amb l’ocupació permanent, entre el 3 i el 24 d’agost, del carril lent dels dos sentits de circulació del tronc central de la ronda, entre les sortides 5 i 6. També es mantindran els talls nocturns de diumenge a dijous.

Aquesta és la segona fase de les obres de la cobertura de la ronda de Dalt després del tram que es va tapar en els vuit anys de govern d’Ada Colau, uns 200 metres entre el mercat de la Vall d’Hebron i l’Institut Vall d’Hebron. En un futur, la cobertura s’ampliarà amb les fases tres i quatre, entre l’avinguda de Jordà i el carrer de la Granja Vella (fase tres), i des de Granja Vella fins al carrer de l’Harmonia (fase quatre).
sources.headline: La cobertura final del nou tram de la ronda de Dalt pren forma al Vall d’Hebron
